title: Env var mapping: FIO_SIZE → TEST_SIZE passes correctly
detail: setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh exports shell var FIO_SIZE (default 256M) and maps it to container env TEST_SIZE in the Deployment template. entrypoint-fio.sh reads TEST_SIZE (default 256M). The indirect naming is intentional and both sides agree on the container-side name and default.

file_evidence.setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh: line 102: FIO_SIZE="${FIO_SIZE:-256M}"; template line 892-893: name: TEST_SIZE, value: "${FIO_SIZE}"

title: SMALL_FILE_RUNTIME consistently removed from both files
detail: entrypoint-fio.sh removed the SMALL_FILE_RUNTIME variable and its banner/diagnostic references. setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh removed the export, the template env entry, and the safe_envsubst list entry. Removal is consistent across all three cross-file touchpoints — no orphaned references remain.

file_evidence.diff: SMALL_FILE_RUNTIME removed from left side (old) lines 27, 40, and corresponding template/safe_envsubst entries; not present in right side (new)
file_evidence.setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh: export removed; template env var removed; safe_envsubst list at line 192 changed from SMALL_FILE_COUNT SMALL_FILE_SIZE SMALL_FILE_RUNTIME to SMALL_FILE_COUNT SMALL_FILE_SIZE

title: RESULT_DIR default /tmp/fio-results consistent across all three files
detail: Dockerfile.fio:14 RUN mkdir -p ... /tmp/fio-results/data pre-creates the structure. entrypoint-fio.sh:17 sets RESULT_DIR="${RESULT_DIR:-/tmp/fio-results}" and creates RESULT_DIR + DATA_DIR at runtime. setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh template mounts emptyDir results-volume at /tmp/fio-results. All three agree on the result path.

title: NFS_CACHE_WAIT env var passed but never consumed by entrypoint-fio.sh
check: Q3 — env vars passed but no longer read
detail: setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh line 113 exports NFS_CACHE_WAIT (default 0) and includes it in safe_envsubst (line 193). The Deployment template (template line 901-902) passes it as a container env var to the fio-test pod. However, entrypoint-fio.sh never references NFS_CACHE_WAIT anywhere — it is dead configuration that passes through the entire pipeline with zero effect.

file_evidence.setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh: line 113: export NFS_CACHE_WAIT="${NFS_CACHE_WAIT:-0}"; line 193: NFS_CACHE_WAIT in safe_envsubst list; template line 901-902: env var entry

title: TEST_SCENARIO convenience selector not exposed in Deployment template
check: Q3 — env vars passed but no longer read (extended)
detail: entrypoint-fio.sh line 36-41 supports TEST_SCENARIO=(basic|small|ai|all) as a convenience shortcut to set all three scenario booleans at once. The Deployment template does not pass this env var, so users who want only small-file tests must individually set TEST_BASIC=false, TEST_SMALL_FILES=true, TEST_AI_MODEL=false rather than using the simpler TEST_SCENARIO=small. Low severity since the individual booleans are exposed.

file_evidence.setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh: template env section lines 885-909: no TEST_SCENARIO entry

title: validate_path() handles --directory correctly
detail: The run_fio() function (lines 178-189) iterates over all arguments and validates the value following --filename or --directory. The validate_path() function (lines 153-169) resolves the path to absolute form via readlink -f and checks it starts with /mnt/* or $RESULT_DIR/*. Since all small-file tests pass --directory values under /mnt/* (e.g., /mnt/s3/small_files_TIMESTAMP/), the check passes correctly. Edge cases like trailing slashes are normalized by readlink -f.

file_evidence.entrypoint-fio.sh: lines 153-169: validate_path() function; lines 178-189: arg loop checking --filename and --directory

title: All shared default values match between setup and entrypoint
detail: Every env var shared between the two files has identical defaults: TEST_SIZE=256M, TEST_RUNTIME=60s, all three TEST_* booleans=true, SMALL_FILE_COUNT=2000, SMALL_FILE_SIZE=16k, AI_MODEL_SIZE=512M, AI_MODEL_RUNTIME=120s, AI_MODEL_JOBS=4. No default value divergence found.

title: CSI_WRITE_RATE, FIO_TIMEOUT, WAIT_TIMEOUT, NFS_CREATE_DELAY not configurable via setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh env vars
check: Q3 extended — env vars entrypoint reads but setup cannot pass
detail: entrypoint-fio.sh reads CSI_WRITE_RATE (default 50m), FIO_TIMEOUT (default 180s), WAIT_TIMEOUT (default 300s), and NFS_CREATE_DELAY (default 0.02s) from env vars with script-level defaults. However, setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh does not export these variables and the Deployment template does not pass them to the container. Users cannot tune FUSE write throttling, fio execution timeout, mount wait timeout, or NFS creation pacing without editing the Deployment template. Notably, CSI_WRITE_RATE is critical for preventing OOM on FUSE dirty page buildup and FIO_TIMEOUT prevents indefinite fio hangs on fsync EIO — the inability to tune these from setup is a config gap.

file_evidence.entrypoint-fio.sh: line 21: CSI_WRITE_RATE="${CSI_WRITE_RATE:-50m}"; line 24: FIO_TIMEOUT="${FIO_TIMEOUT:-180}"; line 18: WAIT_TIMEOUT="${WAIT_TIMEOUT:-300}"; line 46: NFS_CREATE_DELAY="${NFS_CREATE_DELAY:-0.02}"
file_evidence.setup-seaweedfs-lab.sh: no export for any of these four vars; template env section lines 885-909 omits all four
